pr.title: Make some progress on ninja asm build
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Small tooling/build-system PR aimed at making the ninja assembly build progress further. It adjusted configure.py defaults and flag plumbing for assembler/compiler/linker message style, flipped the asm opt-in flag from --no-asm to --use-asm while still targeting the internal no_asm destination, renamed the MetroTRK mem_TRK assembly object to mem_TRK_text, and removed an sbss-only asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s object from the text object list.
what_changed: Renamed asm/MetroTRK/mem_TRK.s to asm/MetroTRK/mem_TRK_text.s with 100% similarity, indicating a pure filename/object-list organization change rather than assembly content changes.
Updated obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES references $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/MetroTRK/mem_TRK_text.s.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/MetroTRK/mem_TRK.s.o.
Deleted asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s, which only contained .sbss reservations for DVDInitialized and LastState.
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s.o from the dolphin DVD TEXT_O_FILES list, leaving src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c.o in the sequence.
Changed configure.py command-line interface from --no-asm with action store_true/default True to --use-asm with dest no_asm and action store_false.
Changed configure.py default compiler message style from gcc to std and updated the help string accordingly.
Removed the hard-coded linker flag -msgstyle gcc from config.ldflags and instead appends -msgstyle {args.msg_style} to both cflags_base and config.ldflags when msg_style is set.
smart_moves: Used a pure rename for MetroTRK mem_TRK to mem_TRK_text, preserving content while making the object name better reflect its placement in TEXT_O_FILES.
Removed an asm file that only defined .sbss symbols from the text object list, reducing a likely section/object mismatch in the asm/ninja build path.
Centralized message-style handling through args.msg_style rather than hard-coding gcc in linker flags while separately configuring compiler flags.
Kept the internal argparse destination as no_asm while changing the external flag to --use-asm, minimizing downstream code churn while flipping the user-facing behavior.
decomp_lessons: When an assembly file contains only .sbss data reservations, do not blindly include it in TEXT_O_FILES; section-specific content should match the build list it is placed in.
For ninja or generated builds, object-list names need to match actual assembly file paths exactly; pure renames must be reflected in obj_files.mk.
Prefer build flags that are derived from one argparse option for both compiler and linker invocations to avoid inconsistent diagnostics or tool behavior.
A compatibility-preserving argparse pattern is to change the public option name while retaining the existing dest used by the rest of the configure script.
naming_conventions: MetroTRK assembly file renamed from mem_TRK.s to mem_TRK_text.s, suggesting a convention of appending _text for files intended to contribute to the text object list.
The new public configure flag is --use-asm, while the internal argparse destination remains no_asm.
Message style option remains --msg-style and accepts mpw, std, gcc, ide, and parseable, with std becoming the default.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Separate or name assembly fragments according to their section role, as shown by mem_TRK_text.s for TEXT_O_FILES.
Avoid carrying sbss-only assembly stubs in text object sequencing; DVDInitialized and LastState reservations in asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s were removed from the asm build path.
For progress on asm-enabled ninja builds, small object-list hygiene fixes can unblock build generation without touching matched C or assembly bodies.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching --use-asm or no_asm in configure.py to see whether the asm build became the default or gained compatibility aliases.
Search for mem_TRK_text.s references to confirm whether _text naming became a broader convention for section-split assembly files.
Inspect src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c for definitions or BSS declarations of DVDInitialized and LastState after removal of asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s.
Search for other .sbss-only assembly files listed under TEXT_O_FILES that may cause similar ninja asm build issues.
